revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 3.svg|thumb|right|250px|神産み神話（イザナギ・イザナミが生んだ神々） [[media:Creation myths of Japan 3.svg|S...'
revision.text:
[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 3.svg|thumb|right|250px|神産み神話（イザナギ・イザナミが生んだ神々） [[media:Creation myths of Japan 3.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''甕速日神'''（みかはやひのかみ）とは、[[神産み]]において[[イザナギ]]が[[カグツチ]]の首を切り落とした際、[[十束剣]]「[[天之尾羽張]]（あめのおはばり）」の根元についた血が岩に飛び散って生まれた三神の一柱であり、火の神とされる。また、[[武甕雷男神]]と同様に刀剣の神であるとも言われる。『[[日本書紀]]』には[[武甕雷男神]]の先祖であるとも記されているが、その後に[[樋速日神]]、甕速日神、[[武甕雷男神]]が同時に生まれたとも記されている。
[[category:日本の神]] [[Category:火神]]

[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 3.svg|thumb|right|250px|神産み神話（イザナギ・イザナミが生んだ神々） [[media:Creation myths of Japan 3.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''甕速日神'''（みかはやひのかみ）とは、[[神産み]]において[[イザナギ]]が[[カグツチ]]の首を切り落とした際、[[十束剣]]「[[天之尾羽張]]（あめのおはばり）」の根元についた血が岩に飛び散って生まれた三神の一柱であり、火の神とされる。
また、[[タケミカヅチ|武甕雷男神]]と同様に刀剣の神であるとも言われる。『[[日本書紀]]』には武甕雷男神の先祖であるとも記されているが、その後に[[樋速日神]]、甕速日神、武甕雷男神が同時に生まれたとも記されている。
==関連項目== *[[天之尾羽張]] *[[タケミカヅチ|建御雷之男神]] *[[日本の神の一覧]]
{{神道 横}}
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みかはやひのかみ}} [[category:日本の神]] [[Category:火神]]

[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 3.svg|thumb|right|250px|神産み神話（イザナギ・イザナミが生んだ神々） [[media:Creation myths of Japan 3.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''甕速日神'''（みかはやひのかみ）とは、[[神産み]]において[[イザナギ]]が[[カグツチ]]の首を切り落とした際、[[十束剣]]「[[天之尾羽張]]（あめのおはばり）」の根元についた血が岩に飛び散って生まれた三神の一柱であり、火の神とされる。
また、[[タケミカヅチ|武甕雷男神]]と同様に刀剣の神であるとも言われる。『[[日本書紀]]』には武甕雷男神の先祖であるとも記されているが、その後に[[樋速日神]]、甕速日神、武甕雷男神が同時に生まれたとも記されている。
==関連項目== *[[天之尾羽張]] *[[タケミカヅチ|建御雷之男神]] *[[日本の神の一覧]]
{{神道 横}}

revision.comment: 『雲陽誌』において「当社の祖神を甕速日といふ星の神と名つく」と記されている旨を記載。
revision.text:
[[Image:Descendants of izanagi and izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|神産み神話（イザナギ・イザナミが生んだ神々） [[media:Descendants of izanagi and izanami.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''甕速日神'''（みかはやひのかみ）とは、[[日本神話]]の[[神産み]]において[[イザナギ]]が[[カグツチ]]の首を切り落とした際、[[十束剣]]「[[天之尾羽張]]（あめのおはばり）」の根元についた血が岩に飛び散って生まれた三神の一柱である。
『[[日本書紀]]』には[[タケミカヅチ|武甕雷男神]]の先祖であるとも記されているが、その後に[[樋速日神]]、甕速日神、武甕雷男神が同時に生まれたとも記されている。
江戸時代に[[松江藩]]で編纂された地誌『雲陽誌』において「当社の祖神を甕速日といふ星の神と名つく」と記されている。<ref>大日本地誌大系 第27巻 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション コマ番号19[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1194083/1/19]</ref>
== 祀る神社 == *[[星神社 (岡山市)]]（[[岡山県]][[岡山市]]）

revision.comment: Created page with " # {{ill|Iwa-saku|ja|石折神}}<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Ihasaku</ref> # {{ill|Ne-saku|ja|根折神}} - Minor Star God. # {{ill|Iwa-tsutsu-no-O|ja|石筒之男神}} #: The gods above were born from the blood that fell from the tip of the sword on the rocks. # {{ill|Mika-Hayahi|ja|甕速日神}} # {{ill|Hi-Hayahi|ja|樋速日神}} # {{ill|Take-Mikagu-tsuchi-no-O|ja|建御雷之男神}}, also known as {{ill|Takefutsu|ja|建布都神}} or {{ill|Toyo-futsu|ja|豊布都..."

revision.text:
#: the blood that fell from the tip of the sword on the rocks. # {{ill|Mika-Hayahi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:甕速日神]] # {{ill|Hi-Hayahi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:樋速日神]] # {{ill|Take-Mikagu-tsuchi-no-O|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:建御雷之男神]], also known as {{ill|Takefutsu|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:建布都神]] or {{ill|Toyo-futsu|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:豊布都神]] #: The gods above were born from the blood that fell from the blade of the sword. # {{ill|Kura-Okami|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇淤加美神]] # {{ill|Kura-Mitsuha|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇御津羽神]] #: The gods above were born from the blood that fell from the handle of the sword.
Also, from the body of Kagutsuchi, the following gods were born:<ref name="kojiki-p80-81"/> # {{ill|Masaka-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:正鹿山津見神]], emerged from Kagutsuchi's head; # {{ill|Odo-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:淤縢山津見神]], from the chest; # {{ill|Oku-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:奥山津見神]], from the abdomen; # {{ill|Kura-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇山津見神]] (or {{ill|Kura-yama-gi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇山祇]]), from the genitals; # {{ill|Shigi-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:志藝山津見神]], from the left arm; # {{ill|Ha-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:羽山津見神]], from the right arm; # {{ill|Hara-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:原山津見神]], from the left foot; # {{ill|Toya-ma-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:戸山津見神]] (or {{ill|Toya-ma-gi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:戸山祇]]), from the right foot.
===Land of Yomi=== [[File:Green willow and other Japanese fairy tales (1910) (14593788578).jpg|thumb]] Izanagi then decided to bring back Izanami and goes to [[Yomi|Yomi-no-kuni]], the underworld. Crossing the gates to that world, he met Izanami and says to her:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=82–83]]</ref>
{{blockquote|The countries that you and me have created have not been completed yet. Let us return!<ref group="nb">「あなたと一緒に創った国土はまだ完成していません。帰りましょう。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
Izanami replied:
{{blockquote|Too bad you did not come before, for I have eaten in the country of Yomi! [By eating food in the land of Yomi, one ends up stuck being a resident of Yomi. This concept is called, "Yomotsu Hegui (黄泉戸喫)."]<ref>戸部民夫 『日本神話』 29-30頁。</ref> ... however, I will consult with the gods of Yomi. Under no circumstances you may look at me!<ref group="nb">「黄泉の国の食べ物を食べてしまったので、生き返ることはできません」「黄泉神と相談しましょう。お願いですから、私の姿は見ないで下さいね。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
On saying this, Izanami entered the palace of these gods. However, time passed and she did not return and Izanagi began to despair. So he broke one of the tines of his ornamental comb mizura that he wore in the left bun of his hair, lit it in order to light the place and decided to enter the world of dead. He manages to find Izanami but is surprised to see that she lost her beauty and had become a rotting corpse, covered with maggots. Of her body were born the eight Gods of thunder, which were:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
# {{nihongo|'''[[Ō-ikazuchi]]'''|大雷]], from the head of Izanami; # {{nihongo|'''[[Hono-ikazuchi]]'''|火雷]], from her chest; # {{nihongo|'''[[Kuro-ikazuchi]]'''|黒雷]], from her abdomen; # {{nihongo|'''[[Saku-ikazuchi]]'''|折雷]], from her genitals; # {{nihongo|'''[[Waka-ikazuchi]]'''|若雷]], from her left arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Tsuchi-ikazuchi]]'''|土雷]], from her right arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Naru-ikazuchi]]'''|鳴雷]], from her left foot; # {{nihongo|'''[[Fusu-ikazuchi]]'''|伏雷]], from her right foot.
Izanagi, shocked, decided to return home, but Izanami was embarrassed by his appearance and commanded the {{nihongo|Yomotsu-shikome|黄泉 丑女|lit. "horrible women from the world of darkness"]] to chase Izanagi. In his flight, he took the head-dress from his head, and threw it to the ground where it turned into a [[bunch of grapes]]. The Yomotsushikome started to eat them but kept chasing the fleeing Izanagi. So he broke the tine of the comb that he wore in his right bun, and as he threw it to the ground it became bamboo shoots, prompting the Yomotsushikome to eat them and enabling Izanagi to flee.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
However, Izanami decided to release the eight gods of thunder and 1,500 warriors from Yomi to continue the pursuit. Izanagi drew and brandished his [[Totsuka-no-Tsurugi]]<ref group="nb" name="grasp"/> sword to continue his flight. As they pursued him, Izanagi reached the {{nihongo|[[Yomotsu-hirasaka]]|黄泉比良坂]], the slope that descends from the land of the living to Yomi. He took three peaches from a tree that had grown in that place and threw them at his pursuers so that they fled.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanagi commented:
{{blockquote|Assistance to all people when they are tired and face difficulties.<ref group="nb">「人々が困っている時に助けてくれ」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
These peaches were called {{nihongo|Ōkamuzumi|意富加牟豆美命, Ōkamuzumi-no-mikoto]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Finally, Izanami persecuted Izanagi, but he lifted a rock that a thousand men could not move and blocked the slope with it. At that moment, their eyes met for the last time.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanami said:
{{blockquote|If you behave in this way, I will strangle and kill one thousand men of your land in one day!<ref group="nb">「私はこれから毎日、一日に千人ずつ殺そう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
Izanagi replied:
{{blockquote|If you do this, I will in one day set up 1,500 parturition houses. So in one day indeed 1,000 men will die and indeed 1,500 are going to be born.<ref group="nb">「それなら私は人間が決して滅びないよう、一日に千五百人生ませよう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
These words justified the circle of life and death in humans. For the same reason, Izanami is also called {{nihongo|'''Yomotsu-ōkami'''|黄泉津大神]] or {{nihongo|'''Chishiki-no-ōkami'''|道敷大神]] and the boulder that covers the entrance to the world of the dead is known as {{nihongo|'''Chika'eshi-no-ōkami'''|道返之大神]] or {{nihongo|'''Yomido-no-ōkami'''|黄泉戸大神]] ō and is today known as {{nihongo|slope of Ifuya|伊賦夜坂, Ifuya-saka]] in [[Izumo, Shimane|Izumo]], [[Shimane Prefecture]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
===Purification of Izanagi=== [[File:Descendants of Izanagi alone-en.svg|thumb|300px|Gods that emerged during the purification of Izanagi.]] Leaving Yomi, Izanagi decided to remove all [[Kegare|uncleanness]] in his body through a purification ceremony (''[[misogi]]'') consisting of a bath in the river at Ahakihara in Tachibana no Ono in Tsukushi. As he stripped his clothes and accessories on the floor the following twelve gods are born:<ref name="kojiki-p84-85">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=84–85]]</ref>
# [[Tsukitatsu funato]] (衝立船戸神 – Post at the Road Bend<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tsukitatsufunato • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/tsukitatsufunato/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>) = Chimata no Kami, emerges from the staff. # [[Michi no nagachiha]] (道之長乳歯神 – Long Winding Way Stones<ref>{{Cite web|title=Michinonagachiha • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/michinonagachiha/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the [[Obi (sash)|obi]]. # [[Tokihakashi]] (時量師神 – Time Keeper Loosed<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tokihakashi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/tokihakashi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the handbag. # [[Wazurai no ushi]] (和豆良比能宇斯能神 – Master Miasma<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wazurainoushi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wazurainoushi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>),<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Wazurahinoushi</ref> from cloths. # [[Michi mata]] (道俣神 – Road Fork<ref>{{Cite web|title=Chimata • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/chimata/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the [[hakama]]. # [[Akigui no ushi]] (飽咋之宇斯能神 – Master Filled Full<ref>{{Cite web|title=Akiguinoushi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/akiguinoushi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the crown [[Corona (disambiguation)|corona]]. # [[Oki zakaru]] (奥疎神 – Beyond Offshore<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okizakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okizakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu nagisa biko]] (奥津那芸佐毘古神 – Offshore Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsunagisabiko/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu kaibera]] (奥津甲斐弁羅神 – Offshore Tide Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsukaibera • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>),<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Okitsukahibera</ref> from the armband of the left hand. # [[He zakaru]] (辺疎神 – Beyond Shoreside<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hezakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hezakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand. # [[Hetsu nagisa biko]] (辺津那芸佐毘古神 – Shoreside Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hetsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hetsunagisabiko/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand. # [[Hetsu kaibera]] (辺津甲斐弁羅神 – Shoreside Tide Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hetsukaibera • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hetsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand.
Subsequently Izanagi is stripped of impurities from the land of Yomi. In this moment two gods were born:
# {{ill|Yasomaga-Tsuhi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:八十禍津日神]], male deity # {{ill|Ōmaga-Tsuhi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大禍津日神]], male deity
Then, shaking off the curse, three gods were born:
# {{ill|Kamuna-obi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:神直毘神]], male deity # {{ill|Ōna-obi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大直毘神]], male deity # {{ill|Izunome (kami)|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:伊豆能売]], female deity
Then, when washing with water the lower parts of his body, two gods were born;
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Sokotsu-watatsumi]]'''|底津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sokotsutsuno'o]]'''|底筒之男神]], male deity
When washing the middle of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Nakatsu-watatsumi]]'''|中津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Nakatsutsuno'o]]'''|中筒之男神]], male deity
Finally, washing the upper part of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Uwatsu-watatsumi]]'''|上津綿津見神]],<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Uhatsuwatatsumi</ref> genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Uwatsutsuno'o]]'''|上筒之男神]], male deity
The trio of Sokotsu-watatsumi, Nakatsu-watatsumi and Uwatsu-watatsumi make up the group of deities called [[Watatsumi|Sanjin Watatsumi]], or the gods of water. The trio of Sokotsutsuno'o, Nakatsutsuno'o and Uhatsutsuno'o make up the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] group of deities, gods of fishing and sea, to whom tribute is paid at [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]].<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
In the last step of the purification ceremony, Izanagi washed his left eye from which the female deity {{nihongo|'''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'''|天照大御神]] was born; washed his right eye from which the genderless deity and spirit {{nihongo|'''[[Tsukuyomi|Tsukuyomi-no-mikoto]]'''|月読命]] was born; and when washing his nose from which the male deity {{nihongo|'''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Takehaya-susano'o-no-mikoto]]'''|建速須佐之男命]] = commonly known as Susano'o was born.<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
With these three gods called {{nihongo|[[Mihashira-no-uzu-no-miko]]|三貴子|extra=”Three precious children”]], Izanagi ordered their investiture. Amaterasu received the mandate to govern [[Takamagahara]] and a necklace of jewels called {{nihongo|Mikuratanano-no-kami|御倉板挙之神]] from Izanagi. Tsukuyomi is mandated to govern over the Dominion of the Night, and Takehaya-susano'o (建速須佐之男命) = Susano'o is to rule the seas.<ref name="kojiki-p86">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=86]]</ref>

#: the blood that fell from the tip of the sword on the rocks. # {{ill|Mika-Hayahi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:甕速日神]] # {{ill|Hi-Hayahi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:樋速日神]] # {{ill|Take-Mikagu-tsuchi-no-O|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:建御雷之男神]], also known as {{ill|Takefutsu|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:建布都神]] or {{ill|Toyo-futsu|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:豊布都神]] #: The gods above were born from the blood that fell from the blade of the sword. # {{ill|Kura-Okami|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇淤加美神]] # {{ill|Kura-Mitsuha|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇御津羽神]] #: The gods above were born from the blood that fell from the handle of the sword.
Also, from the body of Kagutsuchi, the following gods were born:<ref name="kojiki-p80-81"/> # {{ill|Masaka-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:正鹿山津見神]], emerged from Kagutsuchi's head; # {{ill|Odo-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:淤縢山津見神]], from the chest; # {{ill|Oku-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:奥山津見神]], from the abdomen; # {{ill|Kura-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇山津見神]] (or {{ill|Kura-yama-gi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇山祇]]), from the genitals; # {{ill|Shigi-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:志藝山津見神]], from the left arm; # {{ill|Ha-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:羽山津見神]], from the right arm; # {{ill|Hara-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:原山津見神]], from the left foot; # {{ill|Toya-ma-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:戸山津見神]] (or {{ill|Toya-ma-gi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:戸山祇]]), from the right foot.
===Land of Yomi=== [[File:Green willow and other Japanese fairy tales (1910) (14593788578).jpg|thumb]] Izanagi then decided to bring back Izanami and goes to [[Yomi|Yomi-no-kuni]], the underworld. Crossing the gates to that world, he met Izanami and says to her:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=82–83]]</ref>
{{blockquote|The countries that you and me have created have not been completed yet. Let us return!<ref group="nb">「あなたと一緒に創った国土はまだ完成していません。帰りましょう。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
Izanami replied:
{{blockquote|Too bad you did not come before, for I have eaten in the country of Yomi! [By eating food in the land of Yomi, one ends up stuck being a resident of Yomi. This concept is called, "Yomotsu Hegui (黄泉戸喫)."]<ref>戸部民夫 『日本神話』 29-30頁。</ref> ... however, I will consult with the gods of Yomi. Under no circumstances you may look at me!<ref group="nb">「黄泉の国の食べ物を食べてしまったので、生き返ることはできません」「黄泉神と相談しましょう。お願いですから、私の姿は見ないで下さいね。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
On saying this, Izanami entered the palace of these gods. However, time passed and she did not return and Izanagi began to despair. So he broke one of the tines of his ornamental comb mizura that he wore in the left bun of his hair, lit it in order to light the place and decided to enter the world of dead. He manages to find Izanami but is surprised to see that she lost her beauty and had become a rotting corpse, covered with maggots. Of her body were born the eight Gods of thunder, which were:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
# {{nihongo|'''[[Ō-ikazuchi]]'''|大雷]], from the head of Izanami; # {{nihongo|'''[[Hono-ikazuchi]]'''|火雷]], from her chest; # {{nihongo|'''[[Kuro-ikazuchi]]'''|黒雷]], from her abdomen; # {{nihongo|'''[[Saku-ikazuchi]]'''|折雷]], from her genitals; # {{nihongo|'''[[Waka-ikazuchi]]'''|若雷]], from her left arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Tsuchi-ikazuchi]]'''|土雷]], from her right arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Naru-ikazuchi]]'''|鳴雷]], from her left foot; # {{nihongo|'''[[Fusu-ikazuchi]]'''|伏雷]], from her right foot.
Izanagi, shocked, decided to return home, but Izanami was embarrassed by his appearance and commanded the {{nihongo|Yomotsu-shikome|黄泉 丑女|lit. "horrible women from the world of darkness"]] to chase Izanagi. In his flight, he took the head-dress from his head, and threw it to the ground where it turned into a [[bunch of grapes]]. The Yomotsushikome started to eat them but kept chasing the fleeing Izanagi. So he broke the tine of the comb that he wore in his right bun, and as he threw it to the ground it became bamboo shoots, prompting the Yomotsushikome to eat them and enabling Izanagi to flee.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
However, Izanami decided to release the eight gods of thunder and 1,500 warriors from Yomi to continue the pursuit. Izanagi drew and brandished his [[Totsuka-no-Tsurugi]]<ref group="nb" name="grasp"/> sword to continue his flight. As they pursued him, Izanagi reached the {{nihongo|[[Yomotsu-hirasaka]]|黄泉比良坂]], the slope that descends from the land of the living to Yomi. He took three peaches from a tree that had grown in that place and threw them at his pursuers so that they fled.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanagi commented:
{{blockquote|Assistance to all people when they are tired and face difficulties.<ref group="nb">「人々が困っている時に助けてくれ」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
These peaches were called {{nihongo|Ōkamuzumi|意富加牟豆美命, Ōkamuzumi-no-mikoto]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Finally, Izanami persecuted Izanagi, but he lifted a rock that a thousand men could not move and blocked the slope with it. At that moment, their eyes met for the last time.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanami said:
{{blockquote|If you behave in this way, I will strangle and kill one thousand men of your land in one day!<ref group="nb">「私はこれから毎日、一日に千人ずつ殺そう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
Izanagi replied:
{{blockquote|If you do this, I will in one day set up 1,500 parturition houses. So in one day indeed 1,000 men will die and indeed 1,500 are going to be born.<ref group="nb">「それなら私は人間が決して滅びないよう、一日に千五百人生ませよう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
These words justified the circle of life and death in humans. For the same reason, Izanami is also called {{nihongo|'''Yomotsu-ōkami'''|黄泉津大神]] or {{nihongo|'''Chishiki-no-ōkami'''|道敷大神]] and the boulder that covers the entrance to the world of the dead is known as {{nihongo|'''Chika'eshi-no-ōkami'''|道返之大神]] or {{nihongo|'''Yomido-no-ōkami'''|黄泉戸大神]] ō and is today known as {{nihongo|slope of Ifuya|伊賦夜坂, Ifuya-saka]] in [[Izumo, Shimane|Izumo]], [[Shimane Prefecture]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
===Purification of Izanagi=== [[File:Descendants of Izanagi alone-en.svg|thumb|300px|Gods that emerged during the purification of Izanagi.]] Leaving Yomi, Izanagi decided to remove all [[Kegare|uncleanness]] in his body through a purification ceremony (''[[misogi]]'') consisting of a bath in the river at Ahakihara in Tachibana no Ono in Tsukushi. As he stripped his clothes and accessories on the floor the following twelve gods are born:<ref name="kojiki-p84-85">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=84–85]]</ref>
# [[Tsukitatsu funato]] (衝立船戸神 – Post at the Road Bend<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tsukitatsufunato • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/tsukitatsufunato/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>) = Chimata no Kami, emerges from the staff. # [[Michi no nagachiha]] (道之長乳歯神 – Long Winding Way Stones<ref>{{Cite web|title=Michinonagachiha • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/michinonagachiha/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the [[Obi (sash)|obi]]. # [[Tokihakashi]] (時量師神 – Time Keeper Loosed<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tokihakashi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/tokihakashi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the handbag. # [[Wazurai no ushi]] (和豆良比能宇斯能神 – Master Miasma<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wazurainoushi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wazurainoushi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>),<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Wazurahinoushi</ref> from cloths. # [[Michi mata]] (道俣神 – Road Fork<ref>{{Cite web|title=Chimata • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/chimata/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the [[hakama]]. # [[Akigui no ushi]] (飽咋之宇斯能神 – Master Filled Full<ref>{{Cite web|title=Akiguinoushi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/akiguinoushi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the crown [[Corona (disambiguation)|corona]]. # [[Oki zakaru]] (奥疎神 – Beyond Offshore<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okizakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okizakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu nagisa biko]] (奥津那芸佐毘古神 – Offshore Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsunagisabiko/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu kaibera]] (奥津甲斐弁羅神 – Offshore Tide Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsukaibera • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>),<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Okitsukahibera</ref> from the armband of the left hand. # [[He zakaru]] (辺疎神 – Beyond Shoreside<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hezakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hezakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand. # [[Hetsu nagisa biko]] (辺津那芸佐毘古神 – Shoreside Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hetsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hetsunagisabiko/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand. # [[Hetsu kaibera]] (辺津甲斐弁羅神 – Shoreside Tide Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hetsukaibera • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hetsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand.
Subsequently Izanagi is stripped of impurities from the land of Yomi. In this moment two gods were born:
# {{ill|Yasomaga-Tsuhi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:八十禍津日神]], male deity # {{ill|Ōmaga-Tsuhi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大禍津日神]], male deity
Then, shaking off the curse, three gods were born:
# {{ill|Kamuna-obi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:神直毘神]], male deity # {{ill|Ōna-obi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大直毘神]], male deity # {{ill|Izunome (kami)|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:伊豆能売]], female deity
Then, when washing with water the lower parts of his body, two gods were born;
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Sokotsu-watatsumi]]'''|底津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sokotsutsuno'o]]'''|底筒之男神]], male deity
When washing the middle of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Nakatsu-watatsumi]]'''|中津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Nakatsutsuno'o]]'''|中筒之男神]], male deity
Finally, washing the upper part of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Uwatsu-watatsumi]]'''|上津綿津見神]],<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Uhatsuwatatsumi</ref> genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Uwatsutsuno'o]]'''|上筒之男神]], male deity
The trio of Sokotsu-watatsumi, Nakatsu-watatsumi and Uwatsu-watatsumi make up the group of deities called [[Watatsumi|Sanjin Watatsumi]], or the gods of water. The trio of Sokotsutsuno'o, Nakatsutsuno'o and Uhatsutsuno'o make up the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] group of deities, gods of fishing and sea, to whom tribute is paid at [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]].<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
In the last step of the purification ceremony, Izanagi washed his left eye from which the female deity {{nihongo|'''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'''|天照大御神]] was born; washed his right eye from which the genderless deity and spirit {{nihongo|'''[[Tsukuyomi|Tsukuyomi-no-mikoto]]'''|月読命]] was born; and when washing his nose from which the male deity {{nihongo|'''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Takehaya-susano'o-no-mikoto]]'''|建速須佐之男命]] = commonly known as Susano'o was born.<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
With these three gods called {{nihongo|[[Mihashira-no-uzu-no-miko]]|三貴子|extra=”Three precious children”]], Izanagi ordered their investiture. Amaterasu received the mandate to govern [[Takamagahara]] and a necklace of jewels called {{nihongo|Mikuratanano-no-kami|御倉板挙之神]] from Izanagi. Tsukuyomi is mandated to govern over the Dominion of the Night, and Takehaya-susano'o (建速須佐之男命) = Susano'o is to rule the seas.<ref name="kojiki-p86">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=86]]</ref>

#: the blood that fell from the tip of the sword on the rocks. # {{ill|Mika-Hayahi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:甕速日神]] # {{ill|Hi-Hayahi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:樋速日神]] # {{ill|Take-Mikagu-tsuchi-no-O|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:建御雷之男神]], also known as {{ill|Takefutsu|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:建布都神]] or {{ill|Toyo-futsu|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:豊布都神]] #: The gods above were born from the blood that fell from the blade of the sword. # {{ill|Kura-Okami|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇淤加美神]] # {{ill|Kura-Mitsuha|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇御津羽神]] #: The gods above were born from the blood that fell from the handle of the sword.
Also, from the body of Kagutsuchi, the following gods were born:<ref name="kojiki-p80-81"/> # {{ill|Masaka-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:正鹿山津見神]], emerged from Kagutsuchi's head; # {{ill|Odo-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:淤縢山津見神]], from the chest; # {{ill|Oku-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:奥山津見神]], from the abdomen; # {{ill|Kura-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇山津見神]] (or {{ill|Kura-yama-gi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:闇山祇]]), from the genitals; # {{ill|Shigi-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:志藝山津見神]], from the left arm; # {{ill|Ha-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:羽山津見神]], from the right arm; # {{ill|Hara-yama-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:原山津見神]], from the left foot; # {{ill|Toya-ma-tsumi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:戸山津見神]] (or {{ill|Toya-ma-gi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:戸山祇]]), from the right foot.
===Land of Yomi=== [[File:Green willow and other Japanese fairy tales (1910) (14593788578).jpg|thumb]] Izanagi then decided to bring back Izanami and goes to [[Yomi|Yomi-no-kuni]], the underworld. Crossing the gates to that world, he met Izanami and says to her:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=82–83]]</ref>
{{blockquote|The countries that you and me have created have not been completed yet. Let us return!<ref group="nb">「あなたと一緒に創った国土はまだ完成していません。帰りましょう。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
Izanami replied:
{{blockquote|Too bad you did not come before, for I have eaten in the country of Yomi! [By eating food in the land of Yomi, one ends up stuck being a resident of Yomi. This concept is called, "Yomotsu Hegui (黄泉戸喫)."]<ref>戸部民夫 『日本神話』 29-30頁。</ref> ... however, I will consult with the gods of Yomi. Under no circumstances you may look at me!<ref group="nb">「黄泉の国の食べ物を食べてしまったので、生き返ることはできません」「黄泉神と相談しましょう。お願いですから、私の姿は見ないで下さいね。」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
On saying this, Izanami entered the palace of these gods. However, time passed and she did not return and Izanagi began to despair. So he broke one of the tines of his ornamental comb mizura that he wore in the left bun of his hair, lit it in order to light the place and decided to enter the world of dead. He manages to find Izanami but is surprised to see that she lost her beauty and had become a rotting corpse, covered with maggots. Of her body were born the eight Gods of thunder, which were:<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
# {{nihongo|'''[[Ō-ikazuchi]]'''|大雷]], from the head of Izanami; # {{nihongo|'''[[Hono-ikazuchi]]'''|火雷]], from her chest; # {{nihongo|'''[[Kuro-ikazuchi]]'''|黒雷]], from her abdomen; # {{nihongo|'''[[Saku-ikazuchi]]'''|折雷]], from her genitals; # {{nihongo|'''[[Waka-ikazuchi]]'''|若雷]], from her left arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Tsuchi-ikazuchi]]'''|土雷]], from her right arm; # {{nihongo|'''[[Naru-ikazuchi]]'''|鳴雷]], from her left foot; # {{nihongo|'''[[Fusu-ikazuchi]]'''|伏雷]], from her right foot.
Izanagi, shocked, decided to return home, but Izanami was embarrassed by his appearance and commanded the {{nihongo|Yomotsu-shikome|黄泉 丑女|lit. "horrible women from the world of darkness"]] to chase Izanagi. In his flight, he took the head-dress from his head, and threw it to the ground where it turned into a [[bunch of grapes]]. The Yomotsushikome started to eat them but kept chasing the fleeing Izanagi. So he broke the tine of the comb that he wore in his right bun, and as he threw it to the ground it became bamboo shoots, prompting the Yomotsushikome to eat them and enabling Izanagi to flee.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
However, Izanami decided to release the eight gods of thunder and 1,500 warriors from Yomi to continue the pursuit. Izanagi drew and brandished his [[Totsuka-no-Tsurugi]]<ref group="nb" name="grasp"/> sword to continue his flight. As they pursued him, Izanagi reached the {{nihongo|[[Yomotsu-hirasaka]]|黄泉比良坂]], the slope that descends from the land of the living to Yomi. He took three peaches from a tree that had grown in that place and threw them at his pursuers so that they fled.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanagi commented:
{{blockquote|Assistance to all people when they are tired and face difficulties.<ref group="nb">「人々が困っている時に助けてくれ」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
These peaches were called {{nihongo|Ōkamuzumi|意富加牟豆美命, Ōkamuzumi-no-mikoto]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Finally, Izanami persecuted Izanagi, but he lifted a rock that a thousand men could not move and blocked the slope with it. At that moment, their eyes met for the last time.<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
Izanami said:
{{blockquote|If you behave in this way, I will strangle and kill one thousand men of your land in one day!<ref group="nb">「私はこれから毎日、一日に千人ずつ殺そう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
Izanagi replied:
{{blockquote|If you do this, I will in one day set up 1,500 parturition houses. So in one day indeed 1,000 men will die and indeed 1,500 are going to be born.<ref group="nb">「それなら私は人間が決して滅びないよう、一日に千五百人生ませよう」. ''[[Kojiki]]''</ref>]]
These words justified the circle of life and death in humans. For the same reason, Izanami is also called {{nihongo|'''Yomotsu-ōkami'''|黄泉津大神]] or {{nihongo|'''Chishiki-no-ōkami'''|道敷大神]] and the boulder that covers the entrance to the world of the dead is known as {{nihongo|'''Chika'eshi-no-ōkami'''|道返之大神]] or {{nihongo|'''Yomido-no-ōkami'''|黄泉戸大神]] ō and is today known as {{nihongo|slope of Ifuya|伊賦夜坂, Ifuya-saka]] in [[Izumo, Shimane|Izumo]], [[Shimane Prefecture]].<ref name="kojiki-p82-83"/>
===Purification of Izanagi=== [[File:Descendants of Izanagi alone-en.svg|thumb|300px|Gods that emerged during the purification of Izanagi.]] Leaving Yomi, Izanagi decided to remove all [[Kegare|uncleanness]] in his body through a purification ceremony (''[[misogi]]'') consisting of a bath in the river at Ahakihara in Tachibana no Ono in Tsukushi. As he stripped his clothes and accessories on the floor the following twelve gods are born:<ref name="kojiki-p84-85">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|pp=84–85]]</ref>
# [[Tsukitatsu funato]] (衝立船戸神 – Post at the Road Bend<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tsukitatsufunato • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/tsukitatsufunato/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>) = Chimata no Kami, emerges from the staff. # [[Michi no nagachiha]] (道之長乳歯神 – Long Winding Way Stones<ref>{{Cite web|title=Michinonagachiha • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/michinonagachiha/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>),<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Wazurahinoushi</ref> from cloths. # [[Michi mata]] (道俣神 – Road Fork<ref>{{Cite web|title=Chimata • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/chimata/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the [[hakama]]. # [[Akigui no ushi]] (飽咋之宇斯能神 – Master Filled Full<ref>{{Cite web|title=Akiguinoushi • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/akiguinoushi/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the crown [[Corona (disambiguation)|corona]]. # [[Oki zakaru]] (奥疎神 – Beyond Offshore<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okizakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okizakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu nagisa biko]] (奥津那芸佐毘古神 – Offshore Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsunagisabiko/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the left hand. # [[Okitsu kaibera]] (奥津甲斐弁羅神 – Offshore Tide Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Okitsukaibera • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>),<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Okitsukahibera</ref> from the armband of the left hand. # [[He zakaru]] (辺疎神 – Beyond Shoreside<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hezakaru • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hezakaru/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand. # [[Hetsu nagisa biko]] (辺津那芸佐毘古神 – Shoreside Surf Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hetsunagisabiko • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hetsunagisabiko/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand. # [[Hetsu kaibera]] (辺津甲斐弁羅神 – Shoreside Tide Lad<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hetsukaibera • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/hetsukaibera/|access-date=2021-09-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB]]</ref>), from the armband of the right hand.
Subsequently Izanagi is stripped of impurities from the land of Yomi. In this moment two gods were born:
# {{ill|Yasomaga-Tsuhi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:八十禍津日神]], male deity # {{ill|Ōmaga-Tsuhi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大禍津日神]], male deity
Then, shaking off the curse, three gods were born:
# {{ill|Kamuna-obi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:神直毘神]], male deity # {{ill|Ōna-obi|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:大直毘神]], male deity # {{ill|Izunome (kami)|en}}[[category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:伊豆能売]], female deity
Then, when washing with water the lower parts of his body, two gods were born;
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Sokotsu-watatsumi]]'''|底津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sokotsutsuno'o]]'''|底筒之男神]], male deity
When washing the middle of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Nakatsu-watatsumi]]'''|中津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Nakatsutsuno'o]]'''|中筒之男神]], male deity
Finally, washing the upper part of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Uwatsu-watatsumi]]'''|上津綿津見神]],<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Uhatsuwatatsumi</ref> genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Uwatsutsuno'o]]'''|上筒之男神]], male deity
The trio of Sokotsu-watatsumi, Nakatsu-watatsumi and Uwatsu-watatsumi make up the group of deities called [[Watatsumi|Sanjin Watatsumi]], or the gods of water. The trio of Sokotsutsuno'o, Nakatsutsuno'o and Uhatsutsuno'o make up the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] group of deities, gods of fishing and sea, to whom tribute is paid at [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]].<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
In the last step of the purification ceremony, Izanagi washed his left eye from which the female deity {{nihongo|'''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'''|天照大御神]] was born; washed his right eye from which the genderless deity and spirit {{nihongo|'''[[Tsukuyomi|Tsukuyomi-no-mikoto]]'''|月読命]] was born; and when washing his nose from which the male deity {{nihongo|'''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Takehaya-susano'o-no-mikoto]]'''|建速須佐之男命]] = commonly known as Susano'o was born.<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
With these three gods called {{nihongo|[[Mihashira-no-uzu-no-miko]]|三貴子|extra=”Three precious children”]], Izanagi ordered their investiture. Amaterasu received the mandate to govern [[Takamagahara]] and a necklace of jewels called {{nihongo|Mikuratanano-no-kami|御倉板挙之神]] from Izanagi. Tsukuyomi is mandated to govern over the Dominion of the Night, and Takehaya-susano'o (建速須佐之男命) = Susano'o is to rule the seas.<ref name="kojiki-p86">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=86]]</ref>

: '''Mikahayahi''' '''no Kami''' [[ja:甕速日神]] [[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Kami born from Izanagi and Izanami ([[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG format (supported browsers only)]])]]
'''Mikahayahi no Kami''' (甕速日神) is a deity in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, born during the {{ill|kami birth (Shinto)|ja|神産み}} when {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} decapitated {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|カグツチ}}. Mikahayahi no Kami emerged from the blood that splattered onto rocks from the base of the {{ill|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|ja|十束剣}} known as "{{ill|Ame-no-Ohabari|ja|天之尾羽張}}."
According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', Mikahayahi no Kami is considered an ancestor of {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕雷男神}}. However, the text also states that {{ill|Hisahayahi no Kami|ja|樋速日神}}, Mikahayahi no Kami, and Takemikazuchi were born simultaneously.
In the ''{{ill|Un'yōshi|ja|雲陽誌}}'', a regional record compiled during the Edo period in the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}, the section on {{ill|Matsuzaki Shrine|ja|松崎神社}} mentions, "The ancestral deity of this shrine is called Mikahayahi, the god of the stars."<ref>{{cite book|editor=Ashida Ikuto |title=Great Japanese Gazetteer Series|volume=Vol. 27|publisher=Yuzankaku |year=1930 |chapter=Un'yōshi Volume 1, Shimane District|section=Matsuzaki Shrine|page=24|doi=10.11501/1194083|url={{NDLDC|1194083/19}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション}})</ref>
== Shrines Enshrining Mikahayahi no Kami ==
* {{ill|Hoshi Shrine (Okayama)|ja|星神社 (岡山市)}} ({{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山県}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}})

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甕速日神]][[zh:甕速日神]] {{Infobox deity|parents={{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658}}|offspring={{ill|Takemikazuchi|qid=Q1502690}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11574224]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Kami born from Izanagi and Izanami ([[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG format (supported browsers only)]])]] : '''Mikahayahi''' '''no Kami''' (甕速日神) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], born during the [[:en:Kamiumi|kami birth (Shinto)]] when [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] decapitated [[:en:Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]]. Mikahayahi no Kami emerged from the blood that splattered onto rocks from the base of the [[:en:Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi]] known as "[[:en:Ame-no-ohabari|Ame-no-ohabari]]."

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甕速日神]][[zh:甕速日神]] {{Infobox deity|parents={{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658}}|offspring={{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11574224]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Kami born from Izanagi and Izanami ([[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG format (supported browsers only)]])]] : '''Mikahayahi''' '''no Kami''' (甕速日神) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], born during the [[:en:Kamiumi|kami birth (Shinto)]] when [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] decapitated [[:en:Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]]. Mikahayahi no Kami emerged from the blood that splattered onto rocks from the base of the [[:en:Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi]] known as "[[:en:Ame-no-ohabari|Ame-no-ohabari]]."
According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', Mikahayahi no Kami is considered an ancestor of {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕雷男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, the text also states that {{ill|Hisahayahi no Kami|ja|樋速日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisahayahi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mikahayahi no Kami, and Takemikazuchi were born simultaneously.
In the ''{{ill|Un'yōshi|ja|雲陽誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un'yōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', a regional record compiled during the Edo period in the [[:en:Matsue Domain|Matsue Domain]], the section on {{ill|Matsuzaki Shrine|ja|松崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuzaki Shrine|1=Matsusaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11530226}} mentions, "The ancestral deity of this shrine is called Mikahayahi, the god of the stars."<ref>{{cite book|editor=Ashida Ikuto |title=Great Japanese Gazetteer Series|volume=Vol. 27|publisher=Yuzankaku |year=1930 |chapter=Un'yōshi Volume 1, Shimane District|section=Matsuzaki Shrine|page=24|doi=10.11501/1194083|url={{NDLDC|1194083/19}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>
== Shrines Enshrining Mikahayahi no Kami ==
* {{ill|Hoshi Shrine (Okayama)|ja|星神社 (岡山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshi Shrine (Okayama)|1=Hoshi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512982}} ([[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]])
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== See Also ==
* [[:en:Ame-no-ohabari|Ame-no-ohabari]] * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]]
{{Japanese Mythology}} {{Shinto}}
{{translated page|ja|甕速日神|version=101093342|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甕速日神]][[zh:甕速日神]] {{Infobox deity|parents={{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658}}|offspring={{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11574224]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Kami born from Izanagi and Izanami ([[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG format (supported browsers only)]])]] : '''Mikahayahi''' '''no Kami''' (甕速日神) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], born during the [[:en:Kamiumi|kami birth (Shinto)]] when [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] decapitated [[:en:Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]]. Mikahayahi no Kami emerged from the blood that splattered onto rocks from the base of the [[:en:Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi]] known as "[[:en:Ame-no-ohabari|Ame-no-ohabari]]."
According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', Mikahayahi no Kami is considered an ancestor of {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕雷男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, the text also states that {{ill|Hisahayahi no Kami|ja|樋速日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisahayahi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mikahayahi no Kami, and Takemikazuchi were born simultaneously.
In the ''{{ill|Un'yōshi|ja|雲陽誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un'yōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', a regional record compiled during the Edo period in the [[:en:Matsue Domain|Matsue Domain]], the section on {{ill|Matsuzaki Shrine|ja|松崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuzaki Shrine|1=Matsusaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11530226}} mentions, "The ancestral deity of this shrine is called Mikahayahi, the god of the stars."<ref>{{cite book|editor=Ashida Ikuto |title=Great Japanese Gazetteer Series|volume=Vol. 27|publisher=Yuzankaku |year=1930 |chapter=Un'yōshi Volume 1, Shimane District|section=Matsuzaki Shrine|page=24|doi=10.11501/1194083|url={{NDLDC|1194083/19}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甕速日神]][[zh:甕速日神]] {{Infobox deity|parents={{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658}}|offspring={{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11574224]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Kami born from Izanagi and Izanami ([[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG format (supported browsers only)]])]] : '''Mikahayahi''' '''no Kami''' (甕速日神) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], born during the [[:en:Kamiumi|kami birth (Shinto)]] when [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] decapitated [[:en:Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]]. Mikahayahi no Kami emerged from the blood that splattered onto rocks from the base of the [[:en:Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi]] known as "[[:en:Ame-no-ohabari|Ame-no-ohabari]]."
According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', Mikahayahi no Kami is considered an ancestor of {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕雷男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, the text also states that {{ill|Hisahayahi no Kami|ja|樋速日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisahayahi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mikahayahi no Kami, and Takemikazuchi were born simultaneously.
In the ''{{ill|Un'yōshi|ja|雲陽誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un'yōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', a regional record compiled during the Edo period in the [[:en:Matsue Domain|Matsue Domain]], the section on {{ill|Matsuzaki Shrine|ja|松崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuzaki Shrine|1=Matsusaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11530226}} mentions, "The ancestral deity of this shrine is called Mikahayahi, the god of the stars."<ref>{{cite book|editor=Ashida Ikuto |title=Great Japanese Gazetteer Series|volume=Vol. 27|publisher=Yuzankaku |year=1930 |chapter=Un'yōshi Volume 1, Shimane District|section=Matsuzaki Shrine|page=24|doi=10.11501/1194083|url={{NDLDC|1194083/19}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甕速日神]][[zh:甕速日神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甕速日神]][[zh:甕速日神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甕速日神]][[zh:甕速日神]] {{Infobox deity|parents={{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658}}|offspring={{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11574224]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Kami born from Izanagi and Izanami ([[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG format (supported browsers only)]])]] : '''Mikahayahi''' '''no Kami''' (甕速日神) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], born during the [[:en:Kamiumi|kami birth (Shinto)]] when [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] decapitated [[:en:Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]]. Mikahayahi no Kami emerged from the blood that splattered onto rocks from the base of the [[:en:Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi]] known as "[[:en:Ame-no-ohabari|Ame-no-ohabari]]."
According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', Mikahayahi no Kami is considered an ancestor of {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕雷男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, the text also states that {{ill|Hisahayahi no Kami|ja|樋速日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisahayahi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mikahayahi no Kami, and Takemikazuchi were born simultaneously.
In the ''{{ill|Un'yōshi|ja|雲陽誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un'yōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', a regional record compiled during the Edo period in the [[:en:Matsue Domain|Matsue Domain]], the section on {{ill|Matsuzaki Shrine|ja|松崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuzaki Shrine|1=Matsusaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11530226}} mentions, "The ancestral deity of this shrine is called Mikahayahi, the god of the stars."<ref>{{cite book|editor=Ashida Ikuto |title=Great Japanese Gazetteer Series|volume=Vol. 27|publisher=Yuzankaku |year=1930 |chapter=Un'yōshi Volume 1, Shimane District|section=Matsuzaki Shrine|page=24|doi=10.11501/1194083|url={{NDLDC|1194083/19}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>
== Shrines Enshrining Mikahayahi no Kami ==
* {{ill|Hoshi Shrine (Okayama)|ja|星神社 (岡山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshi Shrine (Okayama)|1=Hoshi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512982}} ([[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]])

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甕速日神]][[zh:甕速日神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甕速日神]][[zh:甕速日神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甕速日神]][[zh:甕速日神]] {{Infobox deity|parents={{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658}}|offspring={{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690}}}} [[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Kami born from Izanagi and Izanami ([[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG format (supported browsers only)]])]] : '''Mikahayahi''' '''no Kami''' (甕速日神) is a deity in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}, born during the {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=kami birth (Shinto)}} when {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi}} decapitated {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi}}. Mikahayahi no Kami emerged from the blood that splattered onto rocks from the base of the {{ill|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|en|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|WD=Q2628318|lt=Totsuka-no-Tsurugi}} known as "{{ill|Ame-no-ohabari|en|Ame-no-ohabari|WD=Q10939046|lt=Ame-no-ohabari}}."
According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', Mikahayahi no Kami is considered an ancestor of {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕雷男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, the text also states that {{ill|Hisahayahi no Kami|ja|樋速日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisahayahi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mikahayahi no Kami, and Takemikazuchi were born simultaneously.
In the ''{{ill|Un'yōshi|ja|雲陽誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un'yōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', a regional record compiled during the Edo period in the {{ill|Matsue Domain|en|Matsue Domain|WD=Q1017072|lt=Matsue Domain}}, the section on {{ill|Matsuzaki Shrine|ja|松崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuzaki Shrine|1=Matsusaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11530226}} mentions, "The ancestral deity of this shrine is called Mikahayahi, the god of the stars."<ref>{{cite book|editor=Ashida Ikuto |title=Great Japanese Gazetteer Series|volume=Vol. 27|publisher=Yuzankaku |year=1930 |chapter=Un'yōshi Volume 1, Shimane District|section=Matsuzaki Shrine|page=24|doi=10.11501/1194083|url={{NDLDC|1194083/19}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>
== Shrines Enshrining Mikahayahi no Kami ==
* {{ill|Hoshi Shrine (Okayama)|ja|星神社 (岡山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshi Shrine (Okayama)|1=Hoshi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512982}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama City}})
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ame-no-ohabari|en|Ame-no-ohabari|WD=Q10939046|lt=Ame-no-ohabari}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甕速日神]][[zh:甕速日神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甕速日神]][[zh:甕速日神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甕速日神]][[zh:甕速日神]] {{Infobox deity|parents={{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}|offspring={{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|1=Takemikazuchi}}}} [[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Kami born from Izanagi and Izanami ([[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG format (supported browsers only)]])]] : '''Mikahayahi''' '''no Kami''' (甕速日神) is a deity in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}, born during the {{ill|Kamiumi|en|Kamiumi|WD=Q3545159|lt=kami birth (Shinto)|1=Kamiumi}} when {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}} decapitated {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}. Mikahayahi no Kami emerged from the blood that splattered onto rocks from the base of the {{ill|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|en|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|WD=Q2628318|lt=Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|1=Totsuka-no-Tsurugi}} known as "{{ill|Ame-no-ohabari|en|Ame-no-ohabari|WD=Q10939046|lt=Ame-no-ohabari|1=Ame-no-ohabari}}."
According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', Mikahayahi no Kami is considered an ancestor of {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕雷男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, the text also states that {{ill|Hisahayahi no Kami|ja|樋速日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisahayahi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mikahayahi no Kami, and Takemikazuchi were born simultaneously.
In the ''{{ill|Un'yōshi|ja|雲陽誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Un'yōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', a regional record compiled during the Edo period in the {{ill|Matsue Domain|en|Matsue Domain|WD=Q1017072|lt=Matsue Domain|1=Matsue Domain}}, the section on {{ill|Matsuzaki Shrine|ja|松崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuzaki Shrine|1=Matsusaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11530226}} mentions, "The ancestral deity of this shrine is called Mikahayahi, the god of the stars."<ref>{{cite book|editor=Ashida Ikuto |title=Great Japanese Gazetteer Series|volume=Vol. 27|publisher=Yuzankaku |year=1930 |chapter=Un'yōshi Volume 1, Shimane District|section=Matsuzaki Shrine|page=24|doi=10.11501/1194083|url={{NDLDC|1194083/19}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>
== Shrines Enshrining Mikahayahi no Kami ==
* {{ill|Hoshi Shrine (Okayama)|ja|星神社 (岡山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshi Shrine (Okayama)|1=Hoshi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512982}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama City|1=Okayama}})
